questions.q: The structure of PCC is
questions.solution:
Pyridinium chlorochromate (PCC) can be prepared by the dissolution of chromium trioxide in aqueous $\mathrm{HCl}$. Addition of pyridine gives pridinium chlorochromate as orange cryotol.
PCC offers the advantage of the selective oxidation of alcohols to aldehydes, whereas many other reagents are less selective.

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Identify the products $(X, Y)$ and reaction mechanism $(Z)$ of the following reaction?
questions.solution:
(i) Reaction of ether with HI give an alcohol and alkyl iodide $(R-\mathrm{I})$, in which - I is bonded with more substituted part of ether in case of presence of $3^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$-atom in that part of ether. Thus, the given reaction occurs as follows:
(ii) In case presence of $3^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$-atom, the atom $-I$ combines via $\mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{N}} 1$ mechanism, as $3^{\circ}$ carbocation (formed during the reaction) is highly stable (more than $1^{\circ}$ and $2^{\circ}$ carbocations) and do not follow $S_N 2$ mechanism due to steric-hindrance.
Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: A compound <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>'</mo></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>7</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>8</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is insoluble in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NaHCO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> solution but dissolve in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> and give a characteristic colour with neutral <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>FeCl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> solution. When treated with Bromine water compound <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>'</mo></math> forms the compound <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> with the formula <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>7</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>5</mn></msub><msub><mi>OBr</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>.</mo></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>'</mo></math> is
questions.solution: The compound A is insoluble in sodium bicarbonate solution means, the functional group present in the given compound is alcohol or phenol. Phenols give the violet colour with neutral ferric chloride. Based on these test, the compound can be identified as cresol. The cresol which gives, tribromo product is meta cresol.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q:
Phenols are generally not changed with NaBH4/H3O⊕ 1,3- and 1,4-benzenediols and 1,3,5-benzenetriols are unchanged under these conditions. However, 1,3,5-benzenetriol (phloroglucinol) gives a high yield of product (B)



The compound (B) is:
options: Both and
questions.solution: Phloroglucinol is an organic compound with the formula C6H3(OH)3. It is a colorless solid. It is used in the synthesis of pharmaceuticals and explosives. Phloroglucinol is one of three isomeric benzenetriols.

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An organic compound <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>6</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>7</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> on reaction with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NaNO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>/</mo><mi>HCl</mi></math> at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>273</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>278</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math> followed by warming with water gave <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>.</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></math> reacts with concentration <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>HNO</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> to give <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math>. What is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>?</mo></math>
questions.solution: C6H7N corresponds to aniline (A). Aniline undergoes diazotisation reaction to give diazonium salt which on hydrolysis produces phenol (B). With concentrated nitric acid, phenol is converted to 2,4,6-trinitrophenol (C). The product is commonly known as picric acid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An isomer A of C6H13Cl on hydrolysis by moist silver oxide gives B. The compound B on oxidation gives C. The compound C on esterification with ethyl alcohol gives ethyl-2,2-dimethyl butanoate. Which of the following is A?
questions.solution:
There are 3 steps involved in this question

1 Hydration of alkyl halides in the presence of moist Silver Oxide.

2 Oxidation of alcohol to Carboxylic acid.

3 Esterification reaction to produce the final product.

In the first step Halide ion gets substituted by Hydroxyl ion which results in the formation of alcohol.In the second step alcohol is turned into carboxylic acid and in the last step esterification of carboxylic acid happens.

 

Name of ester is ethyl-2,2-dimethyl butanoate where ethyl is obtained from alcohol and 2,2-dimethylbutanoate is obtained from carboxylic acid. So, name of carboxylic acid is 2,2-dimethylbutanoic acid. This acid is obtained from oxidation of alcohol 2,2- dimethylbutanol and alcohol is obtained from 1-chloro-2,2-dimethylbutane.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

When reacted with acetic anhydride, in the presence of a trace amount of concentrated <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math>, the compound <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> produces <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> as the major product. The compound <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> is
questions.solution: Phenol undergoes the electrophilic substitution reaction to form salicylic acid, which on further acetylation gives the acetyl salicylic acid called Aspirin.

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: What by product is formed in the process of making phenol from cumene?
questions.solution: Cumene (isopropylbenzene) is oxidised in the presence of air to form cumene hydroperoxide. On treating with dilute acid, it is converted to phenol and acetone.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
What are \(X, Y, Z\) in the following reactions?
\(\mathrm{CH}_3-\mathrm{CH}_2-\mathrm{OH} \xrightarrow{Y} \mathrm{CH}_2=\mathrm{CH}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\)
questions.solution: These reactions are known as dehydration reactions where \(\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4\) and \(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4\) are dehydrating agents. Ethanol undergo dehydration by heating with conc. \(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4\) at \(443 \mathrm{~K}\) whereas \(2^{\circ}\) alcohols by \(85 \% \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4\) at \(440 \mathrm{~K}\) and \(3^{\circ}\) alcohols by \(20 \% \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_4\) at \(358 \mathrm{~K}\).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q:
In the reaction,

C6H5OH →NaOHA →140ºC, (4-7 atm)CO2 B →HClC

Product C is ________.
questions.solution:
Phenol reacts with NaOH  to form sodium phenoxide.

Sodium phenoxide reacts with CO2 to form sodium salicylate.

Product formed reacts with HCl to produce salicylic acid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: An organic compound containing one oxygen gives red colour with ceric ammonium nitrate, decolourises alkaline <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>KMnO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math>, responds to iodoform test and shows geometrical isomerism. It should be:
questions.solution:
Ceric ammonium nitrate test is shown by alcohols. So, the compound should contain an alcoholic group.

R-OH +CeNO36-2→R-O-Ce(NO3)5-2 +H++NO3-

Alcoholic group is oxidised by KMnO4 (strong oxidising agent) and KMnO4 itself gets reduced to K2MnO4. So, its purple colour gets discharged.

For positive Iodoform test, compound should have CH3-C=O group. So in this compound, firstly OH group get oxidised to C=O, then it shows iodoform test.

Ph-CH=CH-CH(OH)-CH3 +I2+OH-→Ph-CH=CH-COOH +CHI3

This compound also shows Geometrical isomerism as there is double bond along which there is restricted bond rotation and both groups on each carbon are also different. So, cis-trans pair exists.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The alcohol that produces turbidity immediately with $\mathrm{ZnCl}_{2} /$ conc. HCl at room temperature
questions.solution: Mixture of anhydrous $\mathrm{ZnCl}_{2}$ and conc. HCl is known as Lucas reagent. Lucas test is used for the distinction between primary, secondary and tertiary alcohols. The tertiary alcohol reacts immediately with Lucas reagent producing turbidity. The secondary alcohol gives turbidity within 5-10 min and primary alcohol do not give turbidity at all at room temperature. In the given alternates, 2 -hydroxy-2-methyl propane is $3^{\circ}$ alcohol, so it is more reactive.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which compound is formed on catalytic hydrogenation of carbon monoxide at high $p$ and high $\mathrm{T}$ in presence of $\mathrm{ZnO}-\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ catalyst?
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{CO}+\mathrm{H}_2 \xrightarrow[\text { High pressure }(673 \mathrm{~K}, 300 \mathrm{~atm}) \mathrm{ZnO}-\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_3]{\stackrel{\text { High temperature }}{\longrightarrow}} \underset{\text { Methanol }}{\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{OH}}$
Methanol is industrially prepared when mixture of carbon monoxide and hydrogen is subjected to high temperature and pressure in presence of $\mathrm{ZnO}-\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_3$ as catalyst.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
